overall_synthesis: The Sealsworn GDD is frozen for game architecture v0. Core fantasy, pillars, loop, mechanics, roguelike sections, MVP scope, platform target, and epic sequence align; remaining warnings are tuning/detail concerns that can be carried into architecture, story breakdown, and playtest planning.

findings.title: Information density
findings.location: gdd.md:50, gdd.md:96, gdd.md:440, gdd.md:660; epics.md:36, epics.md:237
findings.note: Most sections carry design weight, but a few freeze-version phrases still use subjective success language such as fun, meaningful, and satisfying without a local observable definition.
findings.suggested_fix: Keep the current text for architecture, but convert retained subjective phrases into playtest-observable criteria during tuning and story breakdown.

findings.title: Measurability
findings.note: Combat has useful baseline numbers, but technical targets lack device tiers, memory or battery budget, and measurement method. Several economy, node-weight, passive, boss, and outpost values are explicitly deferred.
findings.suggested_fix: Before production planning, add target device classes and measurement methods. Keep deferred economy and boss numbers as story/tuning work unless architecture needs a range now.

findings.title: Traceability
findings.note: The chain from core fantasy to pillars, loop, mechanics, and development epics is intact. Major mechanics map cleanly into at least one epic.

findings.title: Core gameplay concrete
findings.note: The GDD has four distinct pillars, a complete repeatable core loop, and testable MVP win/loss conditions.

findings.title: Out of Scope explicit
findings.location: gdd.md:668-685; epics.md:48-53, epics.md:75-80, epics.md:100-104, epics.md:125-129, epics.md:149-153, epics.md:174-178, epics.md:198-202, epics.md:223-227, epics.md:247-252, epics.md:272-276
findings.note: MVP non-goals are explicit in the GDD and each epic carries local out-of-scope boundaries.

findings.title: Dual-audience and self-contained
findings.note: The documents are readable by design, production, and architecture readers. Sections are extractable via consistent headings and concrete tables/bullets.

findings.title: No engine-implementation leakage
findings.note: The GDD specifies player-facing mechanics, platform experience, performance targets, and assets without engine APIs, class/node names, shader internals, or code patterns.

findings.title: Input fidelity
findings.location: project-context.md:1-18; game-brief.md:35, game-brief.md:168-194; gdd.md:559-571, gdd.md:680-681, gdd.md:695-699; decision-log.md:235-243
findings.note: Core fantasy, guardrails, pillars, mechanics, scope, and narrative decisions are preserved. Source references now use repo-relative paths, and the MVP target is clarified as iOS/Android mobile and tablet plus Windows desktop/laptop with a native mobile packaging path preserved from architecture.

findings.title: Technical Specifications stay GDD-level
findings.note: Technical specifications stay at GDD level: performance, platform behavior, accessibility, offline stance, save/resume, and asset budget.

findings.title: No innovation theater
findings.note: The USPs are concrete design differentiators rather than unsupported novelty claims.

findings.title: Genre compliance
findings.note: Roguelike is high complexity, and the GDD includes the expected roguelike sections: run structure, procedural generation, permadeath/meta progression, item and upgrade system, character selection, and difficulty modifiers. Numeric balance-band depth is carried by Q-2 as a later tuning warning.

findings.title: Game-type cross-reference
findings.note: The content strongly matches the roguelike game type, has an appropriate Roguelike Specific Elements section, and now uses the canonical frontmatter id roguelike.

findings.title: Terminology integrity
findings.note: Game-specific terms are consistent across GDD, epics, and decision log: Oath Shards, Echoes, Seal Fragments, Wardenwork, Consume/Destroy, Larval Avatar, affinities, and class names remain stable.

findings.title: Epic continuity
findings.note: Epic titles, sequence, scope, and playable outcomes match between the GDD, epics.md, and decision log. The epics do not yet contain explicit high-level story slices; the current Includes bullets are adequate for architecture but not a final story backlog.
findings.suggested_fix: Proceed to architecture with the current epics. Expand each epic into high-level stories during gds-create-epics-and-stories.

findings.title: Assumptions Index
findings.note: There are no inline [ASSUMPTION] tags, and the Assumptions and Dependencies section clearly lists remaining production design details.

findings.title: Template completeness
findings.note: No unfilled template variables remain, and all canonical GDD sections are present.

findings.title: Open-items density
findings.note: No phase-blocking design decisions remain. The remaining production design details are expected follow-on work and do not block architecture.

findings.title: Required sections
findings.note: The GDD has enough design detail for game architecture: loop, mechanics, generation constraints, save/resume expectations, platform/package target, asset budget, and epics are present.
